page: Amazon Web Services m8g.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier performance for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with clang, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average category. Large model LLM inference prompt processing ranks as average, while small model prompt processing is weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8g.48xlarge leverages the Graviton arm64 architecture to deliver high resource density and multi-core performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. The high core count and memory bandwidth make this instance suitable for highly parallelized workloads. Recommended use cases include large-scale web serving, multi-threaded database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose applications that benefit from high memory bandwidth and massive parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, while single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile delivers high resource density and parallel processing capabilities. The m8g.48xlarge is suited for multi-threaded database operations, large-scale software compilation, static web serving, and highly parallelized general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.48xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for highly parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density arm64 platform featuring 192 dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for highly parallelized general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, software compilation, and web serving
